notes: The Gentleman captures a 1951 Bechstein upright piano with a warm, intimate, and slightly honky character distinct from any concert grand. Upright pianos have a different physical action and string arrangement that produces a drier, more focused tone with less sustain bloom, making this ideal for singer-songwriter, jazz, indie pop, and vintage film soundtrack contexts. The slight natural inharmonicity and character of the aged instrument give it personality that a pristine grand cannot replicate. CC 11 (Expression) shapes dynamics for soft, intimate passages, while CC 1 (Modulation) engages humanisation for a loose, natural feel. The Felt Pedal QC is particularly useful — engaging a felt strip between the hammers and strings produces a muted, prepared-piano-like quality perfect for lo-fi or intimate arrangements. Mic position blending is key to placing the instrument in the mix, from close and present to more distant and ambient.

Volume.description: Master output level of the instrument

Expression.description: Dynamic expression — primary level shaping for intimate, nuanced playing

Modulation.description: Humanise — randomises velocity and timing subtly for authentic feel

Sustain.description: Damper pedal — sustains notes; shorter natural decay than a concert grand

Sostenuto.description: Holds only currently depressed notes for selective sustain

Soft Pedal.description: Una corda / soft pedal — further softens tone for the most intimate dynamic range

Release Time.description: Note release tail length — controls how quickly notes decay after key release

Attack Time.description: Attack envelope speed — subtle adjustments to onset character

Brightness.description: Tonal brightness — filter the high end for a warmer, more vintage character

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — adds space to the naturally dry upright recording

Quick Control 1.description: Mic Position — blends between close (present, detailed) and room (ambient, distant) mic perspectives

Quick Control 2.description: Tone — overall tonal character from warm/dark to bright/present

Quick Control 3.description: Reverb — additional reverb depth to place the instrument in a larger space

Quick Control 4.description: Sustain Resonance — level of sympathetic string resonance when pedal is held

Quick Control 5.description: Release Noise — level of mechanical noise at key release for tactile realism

Quick Control 6.description: Mechanical Noise — general key and action mechanism noise for vintage authenticity

Quick Control 7.description: Felt Pedal / Soft — inserts felt strip between hammers and strings for a muted, prepared-piano quality

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity, tuning, and timing variation for natural performance feel
